meaning: adv.避开,远离adj.孤零的,冷淡的
examples: Barry had his nose put out of joint by Lucy's aloof sophistication.
When those boundaries are crossed, an American's body will visibly stiffen and his manner will become cool and aloof.
He also idolizes the man, who comes off as devout, aloof, and under considerable strain.
It is general de Gaulle and the bronze casting captures his proud aloof air.
